Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal clause with metaphorical predicate structure
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A situation that has gone terribly wrong or become chaotic
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Kwagh a ve kpa a yange
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common Tiv expression referencing disorder in communal life
First Example Sentence: Kwagh u dedoo kera ga a mba u dedoo u yam.
Second Example Sentence: Tor u ve ga kpishi, kwagh u dedoo ka u eren.
Third Example Sentence: Ortom a eren kpishi ka kwagh u dedoo la.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed noun phrase with metaphorical subject
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone behaving foolishly or without wisdom
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Mngu a ngu ka a ior kera
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Draws from Tiv view of wisdom being centered in the head
First Example Sentence: Ishima ior ga ka u ve ga kpishi.
Second Example Sentence: M ngu eren ga u sha, ishima ior ga kpishi.
Third Example Sentence: A eren kpishi ka ishima ior ga.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Parallel clause structure with contrastive repetition
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone acting hypocritically or contradicting themselves
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Mngu a sha kpishi ka a eren kpishi
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used in Tiv debates to criticize inconsistency
First Example Sentence: U sha ka u eren ka a mba eren kpishi.
Second Example Sentence: M ngu eren kpishi ka u sha ka u eren.
Third Example Sentence: A ve kpishi ka u sha ka u eren.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A problem has deeply entered a situation
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects Tiv metaphor of events entering the stomach of society
First Example Sentence: Kwagh a ior u yam ka a mba kpishi.
Second Example Sentence: M ngu eren ga ka kwagh a ior u yam.
Third Example Sentence: A ve kpishi ka kwagh a ior u yam.

First Example Sentence: U dedoo ka u sha ka a mba kpishi.
Second Example Sentence: M ngu eren ga ka u dedoo ka u sha.
Third Example Sentence: A kpishi ka u dedoo ka u sha.

Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Mngu mba eren kpishi u mngu
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Tiv village storytelling culture often references repeated talk
First Example Sentence: Ama u eren ka eren u ya kpishi.
Second Example Sentence: M ngu eren ga ka ama u eren ka eren.
Third Example Sentence: A kpishi ka ama u eren ka eren.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A troubling issue that burdens someone emotionally
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Tiv metaphor linking stomach with emotion
First Example Sentence: Kwagh u yam ga ka m ngu eren kpishi.
Second Example Sentence: M ngu ve kpishi ka kwagh u yam ga.
Third Example Sentence: A kpishi ka kwagh u yam ga.

First Example Sentence: U eren kpishi ka m ngu eren.
Second Example Sentence: M ngu ve kpishi ka u eren kpishi.
Third Example Sentence: A kpishi ka u eren kpishi.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A situation that has become very difficult
First Example Sentence: A ve kpishi ka a mba eren.
Second Example Sentence: M ngu eren ga ka a ve kpishi.
Third Example Sentence: A kpishi ka a ve kpishi.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A matter that people are discussing widely
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects oral discourse traditions in Tiv society
